condition: "bar_fight_done" in player.flags and not ("doss_deal_struck" in player.flags)

start.text: Ludger Thibodeaux's ship docks at my station trailing hull plate, his droid redecorates my bar, and the whole commercial deck goes dark for the week. If I believed in omens I'd space the lot of you. Fortunately I believe in ledgers, and yours is about to balance mine.

tib_history.text: Since before that ship had a wolf's name or a scrubbed registry. He owes me from back then; I owe him from further back. Neither of us counts which debt is bigger — that's what keeps it working. When his ship limps in broke, the debt gets a new line item. Here it is.

the_job.text: One crate. Cold-chain medical — antivirals, surgical kits, cryo drugs. Its manifest says machine parts and its destination doesn't appear on manifests at all: a landing field on Earth's north shore, inside the blockade. The Duskway's final leg. Your ship self-jumps, your crew sleeps, your droid flies, and on the far side you have a very short window to be somewhere the cordon isn't.

choices.text: The blockade is a Compact naval cordon. That's not a delivery, it's a war crime with extra steps.

why_it_matters.text: The Compact calls it a reconstruction protection zone. What it reconstructs is obedience — they glassed a city to start it and they tax insulin to maintain it. The people on that field aren't buyers, they're the reason the word 'blockade runner' is a compliment out here. You'll never see who receives it. That's the design. That's also the point.

the_pay.text: Repairs on your hull, dock fees waived, and twenty-four hundred in station credit at my counter — spend it before you lift, it's no good anywhere else this month. Armor, guns, drive work, gadgets of debatable honesty. There is no right loadout; there's the run you choose to fly. Sneak it, fight it, outrun it — ships have made that field all three ways. Ships have also not made it.

choices.text: Any advice worth what you're paying me?

advice.text: Three kinds of advice at this counter, priced by reliability. Reliable: the pickets fly standard sweeps — dark and slow beats loud and fast, if your nerve holds. Rumor: a runner last month swore the cordon thins on the river approach. Free, and worth it: whatever you buy, the clock on the far side is the enemy that kills most of them. Wake up moving.

deal_prompt.text: So. Do we have a ledger entry, captain?

deal.text: Done. Crate's aboard before you finish shopping, credit's live at the counter now. One more thing — the second wall, the small one by the memorial. Read the names before you lift. Every Duskway runner does. It's not superstition; it's bookkeeping.

mutations.text: Struck the Duskway deal with Thibodeaux's captain — one cold-chain crate for the Charlevoix field, paid in station credit and old debts. Filed the paperwork under machine parts, filed the rest where I file things like that.
